content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges:
[(0, 1, 3), (2, 8, 3), (5, 7, 3), (1, 3, 5), (1, 4, 7), (1, 7, 9), (1, 2, 10), (4, 7, 13), (0, 9, 21), (2, 9, 22), (6, 7, 23), (1, 6, 25), (3, 5, 25), (2, 4, 27)],

We need to find the nodes linked with node 7 exactly at time 23.

First, we will scan each edge (u, v, t) and collect the other node whenever the time t matches the queried time AND one of the nodes matches the queried node.

For time 23:

* Edge (1, 7, 9) has node 1 and time 23. -> Add node 7.
* Edge (1, 2, 10) has node 1 and time 10 (not 23). -> Ignore.
* Edge (4, 7, 13) has node 4 and time 13 (not 23). -> Ignore.
* Edge (0, 9, 21) has node 0 and time 21 (not 23). -> Ignore.
* Edge (2, 9, 22) has node 2 and time 22 (not 23). -> Ignore.
* Edge (6, 7, 23) has node 6 and time 23. -> Add node 7.

The nodes matched are 1 and 6.

Answer: [1, 6]
